betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Particle Chamber Detectors: Wire Chamber Detector§f

§aWire Chamber Detectors track the path of charged particles inside a chamber!§f
The §bWire Chamber Detector§f (`qmd:particle_chamber_detector:2`) is a detector tile placed inside a §bTarget Chamber§f, §bDecay Chamber§f, or §bCollision Chamber§f. When a charged particle beam passes through the detector, it triggers a §bparticle detection event§f that can be used for diagnostics or recipe progress in some QMD chamber recipes.

§6Recipe§f:
Per the §bNC Infuser§f recipe (graph category `nuclearcraft_infuser`):
- Input: §bEmpty Wire Chamber§f (`qmd:part:7`, 1 unit)
- Output: §bWire Chamber Detector§f (`qmd:particle_chamber_detector:2`)

§6Empty Wire Chamber Crafting§f:
- §bAdvanced Processor§f (`qmd:semiconductor:5`)
- §bBSCCO Superconducting Wire§f (`qmd:wireBSCCO`)
- §bPlatinum Ingot§f (`ingotPlatinum`)
- Other materials (see `QMDCraftingRecipeHandler.java` for the shaped recipe)

§6Detector Family in QMD§f:
- §bSilicon Tracker Detector§f (`qmd:particle_chamber_detector:1`) — uses N-Type Doped Silicon
- §bWire Chamber Detector§f (`qmd:particle_chamber_detector:2`) — uses BSCCO wire + Advanced Processor
- §bBubble Chamber Detector§f (`qmd:particle_chamber_detector:0`) — uses Bubble Chamber fluid

§6Use in Chambers§f:
- Detectors are placed as a §bchamber component§f (TileParticleChamberDetector) inside the multiblock interior.
- Some QMD chamber recipes require a specific detector tier to be present before the recipe can execute.
- Detectors contribute to the chamber's diagnostic readouts (track position, energy, particle ID).

§eTip§f: Build at least one of each detector type for flexibility — some QMD chamber recipes (especially in the §bCollision Chamber§f) require specific detector types to function. The NC Infuser is the universal upgrader, so route it from your §bBasic Processor§f / §bAdvanced Processor§f production line.
